name: Daniel Berrigan
description: Jesuit priest, poet and co-founder of the Plowshares movement. On Sept. 9, 1980 he was one of the Plowshares Eight who entered the GE Nuclear Missile Re-entry Division in King of Prussia, PA, hammered on two Mark 12A warhead nose cones and poured blood on documents. Convicted of burglary, conspiracy and criminal mischief; after a decade of appeals the eight were resentenced in 1990 to time served/probation. (Role as Jesuit priest is widely documented public knowledge.)

cases.institution_name: General Electric Nuclear Missile Re-entry Division (King of Prussia)
cases.sentence: 5-10 years; convictions reversed/appealed; resentenced April 10, 1990 to probation for up to 23.5 months in consideration of time served

name: Philip Berrigan
description: Former Josephite priest and co-founder of Jonah House; among the most prolific Plowshares activists. He took part in the 1980 Plowshares Eight (GE, King of Prussia, PA), the 1988 Nuclear Navy Plowshares aboard the USS Iowa (Norfolk, VA), the 1993 Pax Christi-Spirit of Life action (Seymour Johnson AFB, NC), the 1997 Prince of Peace Plowshares (Bath Iron Works, ME) and the 1999 Plowshares vs. Depleted Uranium (Warfield/Middle River, MD). (His marriage to McAlister and Jonah House role are well-documented; the 1993/1997/1999 participation is documented public record, not detailed on the chronology page.)

cases.institution_name: General Electric Nuclear Missile Re-entry Division (King of Prussia)
cases.sentence: 5-10 years; resentenced April 10, 1990 to probation for up to 23.5 months (time served)

cases.institution_name: Norfolk Naval Station (USS Iowa) — Nuclear Navy Plowshares
cases.sentence: 6 months (maximum)

cases.institution_name: Seymour Johnson Air Force Base — Pax Christi-Spirit of Life Plowshares
cases.convicted: Yes (supplemented from public record; chronology lists action title only)

cases.institution_name: Bath Iron Works (USS The Sullivans) — Prince of Peace Plowshares
cases.sentence: 24 months plus 2 years supervised probation and ~$4,667 restitution (supplemented from public record)

cases.institution_name: Warfield Air National Guard Base, Middle River — Plowshares vs. Depleted Uranium
cases.sentence: 30 months; released Dec. 14, 2001 (supplemented from public record)

name: Carl Kabat
description: Oblate Catholic priest who took part in the 1980 Plowshares Eight (GE, King of Prussia, PA), the Dec. 1983 Pershing II disarmament in West Germany, and the Nov. 12, 1984 Silo Pruning Hooks action at a Minuteman II silo near Knob Noster, MO, where he received an 18-year sentence. He also did the Good Friday 1992 N5 silo action at Whiteman AFB, and (per public record, not detailed on the chronology) later clown-suited silo actions in 1994 and 2006 in North Dakota and a 2011 Kansas City plant action.

cases.institution_name: General Electric Nuclear Missile Re-entry Division (King of Prussia)
cases.sentence: 5-10 years; resentenced 1990 to time served/probation

cases.institution_name: Minuteman II silo, Whiteman AFB (Knob Noster) — Silo Pruning Hooks
cases.charges: Destruction of government property, conspiracy, intent to damage national defense, trespass
cases.sentence: 18 years (later reduced to 10 years); released on probation April 12, 1991; plus 3-5 years probation and $2,932.80 restitution

cases.institution_name: Minuteman II silo site N5, Whiteman AFB — Good Friday Plowshares Missile Silo Witness
cases.sentence: Six months halfway house

name: Helen Woodson
description: One of the longest-imprisoned US nuclear resisters. On Nov. 12, 1984 she was part of the Silo Pruning Hooks action at a Minuteman II silo near Knob Noster, MO, using a jackhammer to damage the silo lid; she received an 18-year sentence (reduced to 12). She also acted as a named co-conspirator (from prison) in the 1987 Transfiguration Plowshares West (Whiteman AFB) and Harmonic Disarmament for Life (Clam Lake, WI) actions.

cases.institution_name: Minuteman II silo, Whiteman AFB (Knob Noster) — Silo Pruning Hooks
cases.charges: Destruction of government property, conspiracy, intent to damage national defense, trespass
cases.sentence: 18 years (reduced to 12 years Nov. 1, 1985); 3-5 years probation; $2,932.80 restitution; paroled June 14, 1993

name: Anne Montgomery
description: Religious of the Sacred Heart sister and serial Plowshares participant. She took part in the 1980 Plowshares Eight (King of Prussia, PA), the 1982 Trident Nein (Electric Boat, Groton, CT), the 1984 Pershing Plowshares (Martin Marietta, Orlando, FL), the 1988 Kairos and Kairos Too actions in CT/RI, and the 1989 Thames River Plowshares (New London, CT). (Her participation in the 2009 Disarm Now Plowshares at Bangor is documented public record; not detailed on the chronology page.)

name: Greg Boertje-Obed
description: Army veteran and Jonah House/Catholic Worker activist with multiple Plowshares actions. He took part in the 1985 Trident II Pruning Hooks (Quonset Point, RI), the 1987 Epiphany Plowshares (Willow Grove NAS, PA), the 1988 Nuclear Navy Plowshares aboard USS Iowa (Norfolk, VA), the 1993 Good News Plowshares (Newport News, VA), and the 2012 Transform Now Plowshares at the Y-12 complex in Oak Ridge, TN. (The 2006 North Dakota clown silo action is documented public record; not detailed on the chronology page.)

description: Activist from Baltimore, MD (listed in some sources as Marie Schmidt); took part in the 1984 Pershing Plowshares action at Martin Marietta, Orlando, FL.

cases.institution_name: Martin Marietta (Orlando) — Pershing Plowshares
cases.sentence: 3 years federal prison, 5-year suspended sentence with probation, $2,900 restitution

name: John LaForge
description: Peace worker from Bemidji, MN; with Barbara Katt carried out the 1984 Sperry Software Pair action, entering Sperry Corporation in Eagan, MN disguised as inspectors and hammering two prototype guidance computers for Trident and F4G systems. Judge Miles Lord gave a six-month suspended sentence and criticized the arms industry.

cases.institution_name: Sperry Corporation (Eagan) — Sperry Software Pair
cases.sentence: Six-month suspended sentence

name: Barbara Katt
description: Peace worker from Bemidji, MN; with John LaForge carried out the 1984 Sperry Software Pair action at Sperry Corporation, Eagan, MN, hammering prototype guidance computers for Trident and F4G systems.

cases.institution_name: Sperry Corporation (Eagan) — Sperry Software Pair
cases.sentence: Six-month suspended sentence

cases.institution_name: Electric Boat, Quonset Point — Trident II Plowshares
cases.charges: Malicious damage to property, criminal trespass (burglary-tools count dropped)
cases.sentence: One year and $500 fine

name: Leo Schiff
description: Took part in the 1984 Trident II Plowshares action at Electric Boat's Quonset Point facility, North Kingston, RI.

cases.charges: Destruction of government property, conspiracy, intent to damage national defense, trespass

description: Took part in the 1985 Trident II Pruning Hooks action at Electric Boat's Quonset Point facility, North Kingston, RI.
